blocks.text: ▪aggressive deployment of broadband home access (10-100’s Mbps) ▪2008: software-defined networking (SDN) ▪increasing ubiquity of high-speed wireless access: 4G/5G, WiFi ▪service providers (Google, FB, Microsoft) create their own networks

• bypass commercial Internet to connect “close” to end user, providing “instantaneous” access to social media, search, video content, … ▪enterprises run their services in “cloud” (e.g., Amazon Web Services,
Microsoft Azure) ▪rise of smartphones: more mobile than fixed devices on Internet (2017) ▪~15B devices attached to Internet (2023, statista.com)

raw_text: Internet history ▪aggressive deployment of broadband home access (10-100’s Mbps) ▪2008: software-defined networking (SDN) ▪increasing ubiquity of high-speed wireless access: 4G/5G, WiFi ▪service providers (Google, FB, Microsoft) create their own networks • bypass commercial Internet to connect “close” to end user, providing “instantaneous” access to social media, search, video content, … ▪enterprises run their services in “cloud” (e.g., Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure) ▪rise of smartphones: more mobile than fixed devices on Internet (2017) ▪~15B devices attached to Internet (2023, statista.com) 2005-present: scale, SDN, mobility, cloud Introduction: 1-85
